mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated FEDFUNDS increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations during elevated FEDFUNDS regimes over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over a three-month window signals weakening labor demand that precedes downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Restrictive monetary policy proxied by FEDFUNDS above 4% combined with decelerating industrial production INDPRO compresses both financial and real economic channels, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In joint high-FEDFUNDS and falling-INDPRO regimes, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In restrictive monetary environments, a month-over-month decline in retail sales RSAFS triggers immediate risk-off equity repricing due to direct corporate revenue exposure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS above 4% regimes, month-over-month RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production INDPRO signals improving corporate output and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts HOUST reflect construction sector strength and durable goods demand, supporting positive broad equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising HOUST is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated CPIAUCSL inflation and FEDFUNDS force markets to price prolonged monetary restriction, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods of CPIAUCSL above 4% and FEDFUNDS above 4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls PAYEMS indicates net job losses that reduce household income and consumption forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences in any 2-year window.
